summary: Excellent audience recording captures a high‑energy 1981 show with smoking Birdsong > Let It Grow, powerhouse China Cat Sunflower > I Know You Rider, and tight jams throughout.
review: The tape delivers crystal‑clear audience sound despite being hand‑held, letting the band's raw energy shine.  Highlights include a blistering China Cat Sunflower > I Know You Rider, a soaring Birdsong > Let It Grow, plus killer renditions of Cumberland, Big Railroad Blues and Shakedown > Lost Sailor > Saint.  Overall the performance is tight, joyous and showcases Jerry’s ferocious solo work.

ai_rating.rationale: Across 14 reviews the average rating is ~4.7 with consistent praise for both show and sound; weighting 70 % performance and 30 % audio yields a strong 4.6.

recording_quality.technical_notes: Crisp, clean capture with good instrument separation; occasional hand‑held crowd noise and minor glitches noted by one reviewer but overall very listenable.

standout_songs: China Cat Sunflower > I Know You Rider
show_quality.setlist_flow: High‑energy first set builds into a jam‑heavy second set; transitions are smooth and the band maintains momentum from opener through extended jams.

band_member_comments.Jerry: Repeatedly praised for scorching solos, powerful lead work and overall ‘ripping’ performance; one note of vocal miscues but no impact on instrumental praise.
band_member_comments.Phil: Mentioned as driving force alongside Jerry on Cumberland and other jams.
band_member_comments.Bob: Referenced humorously with Mickey & Bill “messing with his head”; no specific musical critique.
